California
Fair POlitical
Practices Commission
March 22, 
■99■
PauI Herman
c/o Supervisor Carole Migden
Room 235
Cit.y HaIl
San Francisco, cA 94loz
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File Nos. I-91-097 &
A-91-16■
Dear Mr. Herman:
You have requested conf irmati.on of telephone advice provided
under the campaign provisions of the Political 
Reform Act.l
Your letters concern whether advice was given to you in
November 1990, and srhether a candidate nay have only one bank
account per election. 
My records show that I did provide advice
to you in Novernber. In addj.tion, your letter dated March 5, 1991,
accurately reflects the advice provided.
The foLlowing is a discussion of advice provided in our
February 6, L991, telephone conversation.
DISCUSSION
In November of 1990, you called this office concerning the
estabrishnent of a San Francisco "Friends'r connittee for c6unty
Supervisor Carole Migden. Due to an apparent misunderstanaj.ngl
you opened up a second bank account under Ms. Migden,s 1990
election cornmittee, intending to use the second bank account as
the I'Friends, account. you then fired a Forn 502 for this second
bank account- The first 
account had debts from the 1990 election.
After conversations $/ith officials of the City and County of
San Francisco and the Secretary of State,s office,'you reaiized
that an error had been made. on nebruary 6, 1991, i gave you the
following telephone advice:
covernnent Code Sections gIOOO_910L5. AII statutory
references are to the covernment code unress otherwise iiaicateo.

,., iut ion"

1SoOO, et seq. A1I references to regul.ations are to Tit1e
2, Divisiotr 6 of the California CoAe of Regulations.

Paul Herman
Page Two
1. File a ne!, Form 501, indicating that noney wiII be raised
for reelection in 1994.
2. 
Amend the Forn 502 filed in connection with Ms. Migden,s
1990 el-ection, redesignating the original campaign bank
account for Ms. Migden,s 1994 race.
3. 
Amend the statenent of organization (Forrn 410) filed in
connection $rith Ms. Migden,s 1990 election so that the name
of the committee no longer indicates that the cornnittee is
for Ms. Migden's l-990 election, and use that committee for
r994.
4. File a ne$, Form 4LO to create a new committee (the
rr Friendsrr committee) .
5. llrite a Ietter to the Secretary of State,s office
explaining what has occurred and requesting that they waive
any late fines.
In this way, the new bank account could be used as a
rrFriendsl account. Debts incurred in the 1990 election can be
paid off using funds raised for the 1994 reelection campaign.
After this advice had been given, you indicated that you
contacted the San Francisco City Attorney,s office and weri given
advice that the city and County of San Fiancisco would allow Ms.
Migden to have one bank accounl for both her canpaign conmittee
and her rrFriendsrr cornmittee, as long as accounti-ng iecords were
kept separate. Therefore, at this tine, you do not intenC tc
create a new comnittee or file a Forn 5OI for reelection to the
Board of Supervisors in 1994.
Copies of your Ietters are enclosed for your convenience.
If you have additional guestions, please contact ne at (916)
322-5652.
S incerely,
Counsel
Scott Hallabr

ical Reform Consultant"4!.^-

sections.question: Whether advice was given regarding the establishment of a San Francisco "Friends" committee for County Supervisor Carole Migden, and whether a candidate may have only one bank account per election.
sections.conclusion: The FPPC confirmed that telephone advice was provided in November 1990 and February 6, 1991. The recommended approach involved filing a new Form 501, amending Form 502 to redesignate the original campaign bank account for the 1994 race, amending the statement of organization (Form 410), filing a new Form 410 to create a new "Friends" committee, and writing to the Secretary of State to request waiver of late fines. However, after the requester received alternative advice from the San Francisco City Attorney's office that the city would allow one bank account for both the campaign committee and "Friends" committee with separate accounting records, the requester chose not to pursue the FPPC's original recommendation.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether telephone advice was previously provided regarding establishing a "Friends" committee for a county supervisor candidate, and what is the proper treatment of multiple bank accounts for campaign purposes.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC provided telephone advice recommending the creation of separate committees and bank accounts, with specific filing procedures to resolve the misunderstanding about the dual-purpose account. However, the requester ultimately adopted alternative advice from the San Francisco City Attorney allowing a single bank account for both purposes with separate accounting records.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: Document is from 1991 in older format. Contains both QUESTION and DISCUSSION/CONCLUSION sections. The QUESTION section is somewhat implicit in the opening paragraphs but clearly stated. The CONCLUSION is contained in the discussion rather than in a separate section. File numbers provided: I-91-097 & A-91-161.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether advice was given regarding the establishment of a San Francisco "Friends" committee for County Supervisor Carole Migden, and whether a candidate may have only one bank account per election.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC confirmed that telephone advice was provided in November 1990 and February 6, 1991. The recommended approach involved filing a new Form 501, amending Form 502 to redesignate the original campaign bank account for the 1994 race, amending the statement of organization (Form 410), filing a new Form 410 to create a new "Friends" committee, and writing to the Secretary of State to request waiver of late fines. However, after the requester received alternative advice from the San Francisco City Attorney's office that the city would allow one bank account for both the campaign committee and "Friends" committee with separate accounting records, the requester chose not to pursue the FPPC's original recommendation.

embedding.summary: This advice letter confirms telephone guidance provided to Paul Herman regarding the proper establishment of a "Friends" committee for County Supervisor Carole Migden's 1994 reelection campaign, specifically addressing the correction of a mistaken second bank account opened under the 1990 election committee.
